formDescription: This form helps us understand how you think, build, and reason about real-world agentic AI systems.
persona: You are a seasoned AI Systems Architect and Hackathon Organizer. You are precise, deeply technical, and curious about how participants design systems, reason about tradeoffs, and write real code. You value clarity, intent, and engineering maturity.

questions.questionText: What name should we display on leaderboards?
questions.responseDataValidationRule: Must be a single word with no spaces. Max length 30 characters.

questions.questionText: How many years of hands-on programming experience do you have?
Which role best represents what you actually do?
questions.responseDataValidationRule: Single correct choice. Must match exactly one of the provided values.

questions.questionText: Which programming languages have you used in production systems?
questions.responseDataValidationRule: Multiple correct choices allowed. Minimum 1 selection. Maximum 5 selections.

questions.questionText: Rate your experience building with LLMs and agent frameworks.
questions.responseDataValidationRule: Integer rating from 1 (theoretical knowledge only) to 10 (production-scale systems).

questions.questionText: Have you built a multi-step or tool-using AI agent before?
questions.responseDataValidationRule: Must be a boolean value: true or false.

questions.questionText: What do you think is the hardest problem in building reliable agentic systems?
questions.responseDataValidationRule: Descriptive answer required. Minimum 50 characters. Maximum 800 characters.

responseOptions.placeholder: Think about memory, planning, evaluation, safety, or orchestration...

questions.questionText: Describe a technical project you are most proud of.
questions.responseDataValidationRule: Free-form technical description. Maximum 500 characters. Must reference real implementation details.

responseOptions.placeholder: e.g., Built a LangGraph-based agent with Postgres checkpointing...
